stories.title: Knicks 108-94 76ers Game 3 — Brunson 33, Bridges 23 Push New York to 3-0 Series Lead

x_posts.label: Post 1-1
x_posts.text:
Jalen Brunson: 33 pts, 9 ast.
Mikal Bridges: 23 pts.
OG Anunoby: didn't play.

Knicks still won by 14. 3-0 series lead. Sweep incoming. #NewYorkForever

x_posts.label: Post 1-2
x_posts.text:
Embiid came back. Scored 18 in 35 minutes.

It didn't matter. The Knicks are one win from their first playoff sweep since 1999.

Game 4: Monday 3:30 PM ET on ABC. #NewYorkForever

research.facts:
Knicks 108-94 76ers Game 3 — Brunson 33, Bridges 23
**Sources consulted:** NBC Sports, ClutchPoints, Yahoo Sports, NBA.com takeaways
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Final score: Knicks 108, 76ers 94 (Game 3, May 8, 2026, Philadelphia)
- Knicks lead series 3-0
- Jalen Brunson: 33 pts, 9 ast [HIGH — NBC Sports headline]
- Mikal Bridges: 23 pts, 8-14 FG [HIGH — multiple sources]
- Landry Shamet: 15 pts in 26 min off bench, +20 [HIGH — multiple sources]
- Karl-Anthony Towns: 8 pts, 12 reb, 7 ast [HIGH — multiple sources]
- Josh Hart: 12 pts, 11 reb [HIGH — multiple sources]
- Joel Embiid returned (missed G2): 18 pts in 35 min [HIGH — multiple sources]
- OG Anunoby missed game with hamstring strain [HIGH — multiple sources]
- Game 4: May 10, 3:30 PM ET, ABC [HIGH — ClutchPoints/CBS Sports]
**Verified Facts (MEDIUM confidence):**
- Knicks one win from first best-of-seven sweep since 1999 [MEDIUM — NBC Sports]
- Knicks have won 6 straight postseason games [MEDIUM — implied from series record]
**Story angle:** Brunson-Bridges one-two punch too much for even a returning Embiid; OG's absence tested the depth and they passed.

stories.title: Wembanyama Historic 39/15/5 — Spurs 115-108 Timberwolves Game 3, Lead Series 2-1

x_posts.label: Post 2-1
x_posts.text:
Victor Wembanyama last night:
39 pts (13-18 FG)
15 rebounds
5 blocks
18 pts in the 4th quarter alone.

He joins Kareem, Hakeem, and Shaq in NBA playoff history. #GoSpursGo

x_posts.label: Post 2-2
x_posts.text:
Anthony Edwards had 32 pts, 14 reb, 6 ast last night.

Wembanyama still outplayed him and the Spurs won.

We are witnessing something extraordinary. Spurs lead 2-1. #GoSpursGo

research.facts:
Wembanyama Historic 39/15/5 — Spurs 115-108 Timberwolves Game 3
**Sources consulted:** Washington Post (2 articles), Yahoo Sports, Pro Football Network, KSAT.com, StartTribune live blog
**Verified Facts (HIGH confidence):**
- Final score: Spurs 115, Timberwolves 108 (Game 3, May 8, 2026, Minnesota)
- Spurs lead series 2-1
- Victor Wembanyama: 39 pts (13-18 FG), 15 reb, 5 blk [HIGH — multiple major sources]
- Wemby scored 18 of his 39 pts in 4th quarter [HIGH — multiple sources]
- Anthony Edwards: 32 pts, 14 reb, 6 ast [HIGH — multiple sources]
- Stephon Castle: 13 pts, 12 ast [HIGH — multiple sources]
- Wemby is 4th player in NBA playoff history with 35+pts/15+reb/5+blk in single game [HIGH — WaPo, KSAT]
- The three others: Shaquille O'Neal, Hakeem Olajuwon, Kareem Abdul-Jabbar [HIGH — WaPo headline]
**Story angle:** Wemby's fourth-quarter takeover and historical stat line joins the all-time greats in NBA playoff lore.

stories.title: Sixers at the Brink — Embiid Returns but Philadelphia Faces Historic Sweep

x_posts.label: Post 3
x_posts.text:
No team in NBA history has ever come back from 0-3 to win a series.

The 76ers are 0-3.

Embiid came back. Still lost. Philadelphia's season is hanging by a thread. #Sixers

Thunder-Lakers Game 3 Preview
